reason:
The gap-fix discharges clause (i) '1 in A_+' of GT-orderunit-def by delegating to node 1.2.1 ('cone-membership 1 in A_+ holds by node 1.2.1'), but the support is not properly grounded, on two counts.

(1) MISSING DEPENDENCY EDGE 1.2 -> 1.2.1. Node 1.2's clause (i) is load-bearing and rests ENTIRELY on node 1.2.1, yet node 1.2 still declares 'Depends on: 1.1' only -- it does NOT declare 1.2.1 as a dependency. The af verifier checklist's own BAD example is exactly this: 'Uses result from node X but X not listed as dependency'. Moreover node 1.2.1 is still epistemic=pending (unvalidated): accepting 1.2 would mean validating a node whose load-bearing clause rests on an unvalidated sub-node -- the checklist's other BAD example ('Depends on node which is still pending'). Per af, 1.2.1 IS a child of 1.2 (its proof scope), so the proper grounding is for 1.2.1 to be validated FIRST; 1.2 cannot be accepted while 1.2.1 is pending.

(2) NODE 1.2.1 ITSELF HAS AN OPEN MAJOR CHALLENGE (ch-3165f18bdabf1c37, unresolved -- ledger 000029 is the only entry, no resolution). Its first load-bearing inference 'by node 1.1, 1 in A=Im P' consumes node 1.1's result, but 1.2.1 declares NO dependency on 1.1. Node 1.1 is an UNCLE of 1.2.1 (sibling of parent 1.2), not an ancestor; the parent's 'Depends on: 1.1' does not transitively place 1.1 in the proof scope of 1.2's children, so 1.2.1 reaches outside its declared scope. The validated siblings set the bar explicitly: 1.2 declares 'Depends on: 1.1', 1.3 'Depends on: 1.1, 1.2', 1.4 'Depends on: 1.1, 1.2, 1.3'.

The MATHEMATICS is correct and every cited GT-* byte-verifies against refs/hos/joa-m.md: GT-orderunit-def (line 368) verbatim 'An element e in A^+ is called an order unit for A ...' makes 'e in A^+' definitional, so the order unit 1 of V (GT-jb-orderunit-3310, line 2552 verbatim 'order unit the identity 1') lies in V_+; with 1 in A (node 1.1) and def-near-fixed-algebra (A_+ = A cap V_+), 1 in A_+. So the conclusion is TRUE. But the proof STRUCTURE has a real, non-cosmetic gap. FIX: (a) add the dependency edge 1.2.1 -> 1.1 and validate 1.2.1 (resolving ch-3165f18bdabf1c37); (b) declare 1.2.1 as a dependency of 1.2. Only then is 1.2's clause (i) properly grounded and acceptable.
